title: nigella arvensis var. microcarpa
intro: Nigella arvensis var. microcarpa, also known as lesser caltrop or small-seeded black cumin, is a plant species belonging to the ranunculaceae family. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, due to the presence of active compounds such as alkaloids and flavonoids. The health benefits associated with this plant include its potential to alleviate digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties, which can help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Nigella arvensis var. microcarpa can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, capsules, and tinctures, but more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
